cache_usage_limits.description: Defines the maximum memory usage limits for the serverless cache, specifying the range of memory allocation in GB required for scaling.
cache_usage_limits.type: list(object({data_storage: list(object({maximum: optional(number),minimum: optional(number),unit: string})),ecpu_per_second: list(object({maximum: optional(number),minimum: optional(number)}))}))

daily_snapshot_time.description: Time of day (in UTC) to create a daily snapshot, formatted as HH:MM.

description.description: Provides a brief explanation of the serverless cache, its purpose, or any relevant details for identification or documentation.

engine.description: Specifies the type of caching engine to be used, such as Redis, for the serverless cache.

kms_key_id.description: Specifies the KMS key ARN to encrypt data at rest for the serverless ElastiCache cluster; must be a valid AWS KMS key.

major_engine_version.description: Specifies the major version of the database engine to be used for the serverless cache. Must be compatible with the Elasticache serverless service.

name.description: Name of the Amazon ElastiCache Serverless cache cluster, must be unique within the account.

security_group_ids.description: List of security group IDs to associate with the serverless cache, enabling network access control.

snapshot_arns_to_restore.description: List of Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) for snapshots to restore into the serverless cache during creation.

snapshot_retention_limit.description: Defines the number of days to retain snapshots before automatic deletion; must be a non-negative integer.

subnet_ids.description: List of subnet IDs for the cache, required for VPC network placement. Ensure subnets belong to the same VPC.

tags.description: Key-value pairs to assign to the resource for tagging and identification purposes; supports custom metadata and must comply with AWS tag naming conventions.

timeouts.description: Specifies the duration settings for create, update, and delete operations, ensuring adequate time for provisioning or modifications.

user_group_id.description: ID of the user group to associate with the serverless cache, used for access control; must reference an existing Elasticache user group.
